info.name: Text2SQL Backend — SP26 Group 28
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the Text2SQL FastAPI backend.
Base URL variable: {{base_url}} = http://localhost:8000/api/v1

Import this file into Postman, set the 'base_url' collection variable, and run.

request.description: Lightweight liveness probe. Always returns 200 with status=healthy. Use this for load-balancer / k8s readiness checks.

Deep health check — probes Ollama reachability, database availability, RAG index status, and OpenAI key configuration.

Overall status:
- 'healthy'   → all components ok
- 'degraded'  → some components degraded (e.g. RAG not built yet)
- 'unhealthy' → critical component unavailable (e.g. Ollama down)

response.name: 200 OK — Degraded (RAG not built yet)

request.description: Returns a list of all available SQLite database names under the configured SQLITE_DB_DIR.

Returns the full schema for the specified database: table names, columns (name, type, PK flag, nullable), and foreign key relationships.

Replace 'concert_singer' with any database from the list endpoint.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Embeds all (question, SQL) pairs from the Spider training JSON and writes a FAISS index to disk.

Set 'examples_path' to a custom path, or leave null to use the path from .env (SPIDER_EXAMPLES_PATH).

Must be called at least once before RAG is active. The index persists and is reloaded automatically on next startup.
Reloads a previously built FAISS index from disk into memory. Useful if the server was restarted and the lifespan loader failed for some reason.

response.name: 200 OK — No Index Found
response.body: { "loaded": false, "message": "No index found on disk. Build it first." }

request.description: Quick check: is the FAISS index loaded in memory and ready to serve retrievals?
Development / inspection endpoint. Returns the top-K most similar training examples for the given question. Useful for verifying that retrieval is working before running full generation.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Core endpoint. Accepts a natural language question and returns:
- generated_sql: the SQL produced by the LLM
- executed: whether the SQL ran without error
- execution_result: array of result rows (objects)
- execution_error: error string if execution failed
- repaired: whether the repair loop was triggered
- rag_context_used: whether RAG examples were injected
- retrieved_examples: the examples that were injected
- latency_ms: end-to-end time in milliseconds

model_backend options: 'ollama' | 'openai' | 'seq2sql'

response.name: 200 OK — Success
response.body:
{
  "question": "How many singers are there?",
  "generated_sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM singer;",
  "executed": true,
  "execution_result": [ { "count(*)": 5 } ],
  "execution_error": null,
  "repaired": false,
  "repair_attempts": 0,
  "rag_context_used": true,
  "retrieved_examples": [
    "How many singers are there? → SELECT count(*) FROM singer"
  ],
  "model_backend": "ollama",
  "latency_ms": 342.5
}

response.name: 503 Ollama Unavailable
response.body: { "detail": "Cannot reach Ollama at http://localhost:11434. Ensure Ollama is running." }

body.raw:
{
  "question": "List all concert names and their stadiums",
  "database_name": "concert_singer",
  "model_backend": "openai",
  "use_rag": false,
  "conversation_history": []
}

request.description: Same endpoint but using the OpenAI frontier model. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY in .env.
Demonstrates conversational (CoSQL-style) mode. Pass previous question/SQL turns in 'conversation_history'. The backend injects them into the prompt to maintain context.
Uses the Seq2SQL stub. Returns a placeholder SQL — replace the stub in llm_service.py with real model inference when integrating the actual Seq2SQL model.

body.raw:
{
  "database_name": "concert_singer",
  "question": "How many singers are there?",
  "ground_truth_sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM singer",
  "model_backend": "ollama",
  "use_rag": true
}

request.description:
Generates SQL for the question, then computes:
- exact_match: whether generated SQL matches ground truth (normalised)
- execution_accuracy: whether both SQLs return the same result set
- error_category: if wrong, classifies the error type

Error categories: schema_linking | join_construction | aggregation | nested_query | conversational_context | syntax | execution | unknown

response.name: 200 OK — Correct
response.body:
{
  "question": "How many singers are there?",
  "ground_truth_sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM singer",
  "generated_sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM singer;",
  "exact_match": true,
  "execution_accuracy": true,
  "error_category": null,
  "error_detail": null,
  "latency_ms": 415.2
}

response.name: 200 OK — Wrong (Schema Linking Error)
response.body:
{
  "question": "How many singers are there?",
  "ground_truth_sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM singer",
  "generated_sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM singers",
  "exact_match": false,
  "execution_accuracy": false,
  "error_category": "schema_linking",
  "error_detail": "Generated SQL failed: no such table: singers",
  "latency_ms": 388.0
}

request.description:
Runs evaluation over multiple (question, ground_truth_sql) pairs and returns:
- total: number of examples
- exact_match_score: EM accuracy (0.0–1.0)
- execution_accuracy_score: EX accuracy (0.0–1.0)
- results: per-example EvaluationResult objects

The batch-level model_backend and use_rag override the per-item values.

response.name: 200 OK
